description: A square dark-navy infographic card explaining ICE's three local/state law-enforcement partnership models under the 287(g) program. The background is a mottled dark blue textured field. At top, white serif text reads 'ICE local and state law enforcement' above a large white serif headline 'PARTNER PROGRAMS'. Down the left side are three rounded-rectangle thumbnail photos, each tinted a different color: the top image (red tint) shows what appears to be jail/prison cell bars with an officer; the middle image (blue tint) shows tactical officers in vests; the bottom image (grayscale) shows a hand writing with a pen on paper/documents. To the right of each thumbnail is a bold white subhead and explanatory body text: 'Jail Enforcement Model - ID and process removable aliens you arrest with active or pending criminal charges.'; 'Task Force Model - Allows your officers to enforce limited immigration authority in certain situations.'; 'Warrant Service Officer - Serve and carry out administrative warrants against illegal aliens.' Bottom-left reads 'ICE.gov/287g' in white; bottom-right shows the DHS seal and the wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'. The graphic is a clean, corporate-style explainer aimed at recruiting local police departments into immigration enforcement partnerships, using official agency branding and structured bullet content to convey legitimacy and ease of participation.
notable_text: PARTNER PROGRAMS; Jail Enforcement Model; Task Force Model; Warrant Service Officer; ICE.gov/287g
